specification.topic-statement: The agent history replay system reconstructs a chronologically ordered message sequence from the database by walking the ancestor chain backward and applying conversation clear boundaries to determine the starting point for each ancestor's contribution.

scope.in-scope: Ancestor chain traversal, clear event detection, replay range construction, message range querying, message accumulation, and interrupted-turn marking.
scope.boundary: This topic executes parameterized SQL queries and receives message rows and agent records. The schema layout, indexing, and transactional guarantees are outside this scope.

How the reconstructed message sequence is consumed — which messages are rendered to the scrollback, which are loaded into the live conversation array, and how marks are restored — is handled by the agent restoration subsystem and is outside this scope.

field.name: agent identifier
field.note: UUID of the ancestor agent whose messages this range queries

field.name: start id
field.note: exclusive lower bound; messages with id strictly greater than this value are included; zero means from the beginning

field.name: end id
field.note: inclusive upper bound; messages with id at most this value are included; zero means no upper limit

field.name: id
field.note: database row identifier; used for range boundary arithmetic

field.name: kind
field.note: message type discriminator (e.g., user, assistant, clear, interrupted)

field.name: content
field.note: text payload; null for structural messages

field.name: interrupted
field.note: set to true after filtering if this message is part of a cancelled turn; always false before filtering

field.name: messages
field.note: accumulated messages in chronological order, covering all ancestors

behavior.building-replay-ranges:
Before any messages are loaded, a plan (an ordered list of replay ranges) is constructed by walking the ancestor chain from the target agent upward toward the root.
The walk starts at the target agent. At each step, the most recent clear event in the current agent's history is located, subject to any upper bound imposed by the caller. A clear event is the message with the highest id among all messages of kind "clear" that fall within the applicable range.
If a clear event is found: - A replay range is recorded for this agent, starting after the clear event (start id set to the clear event's id, meaning messages with ids strictly greater than the clear id will be included) and ending at the upper bound. - The walk terminates. No further ancestors are consulted.
If no clear event is found: - A replay range is recorded for this agent starting from the beginning (start id set to zero) and ending at the upper bound. - If this agent has a parent, the walk continues upward. The upper bound for the parent is set to the fork message id recorded on the current agent — the id of the message in the parent's history at which the current agent was created. - If this agent has no parent (it is the root), the walk terminates.
The walk produces ranges in leaf-to-root order. Before queries are executed, the range list is reversed so that ranges are in root-to-leaf (chronological) order. Queries are then executed in that order.
behavior.querying-messages:
For each replay range in chronological order, a single database query retrieves all messages matching the range:
agent identifier = range.agent_identifier AND message id > range.start_id AND (range.end_id = 0 OR message id ≤ range.end_id) ORDER BY creation timestamp
Messages are returned ordered by creation timestamp. If the range contains no messages, an empty result is returned without error.
The fields retrieved for each message are: id, kind, content, and data.
An end id of zero signals "no upper bound" in the query. This means it is impossible to express a range that ends specifically at message id zero; zero is reserved as the sentinel value for unbounded ranges.
behavior.accumulating-messages:
As each range's query results are returned, messages are copied into the replay context's message array. The array grows dynamically: when capacity is exhausted, it is doubled. Initial capacity is zero; the first message triggers allocation to an initial capacity of sixteen.
Each copied message has its interrupted flag initialized to false. The source messages from the query result are not modified.
behavior.filtering-interrupted-turns:
After all ranges have been accumulated, the complete message sequence is scanned once to identify and mark interrupted turns.
The scan maintains a "last user message" cursor. When a message of kind "user" is encountered, the cursor advances to that message's position. When a message of kind "interrupted" is encountered, every message from the last user message position through the interrupted message (inclusive) has its interrupted flag set to true.
Messages are not removed from the sequence. Consumers are responsible for deciding how to handle messages whose interrupted flag is true.
All messages between the user message that started a cancelled turn and the interrupted marker that ended it are marked, including any tool calls, tool results, and partial assistant messages in between.
The replay context includes a mark stack structure that is initialized with capacity zero and count zero. No messages are ever examined for mark content during replay. The mark stack remains empty after replay completes.
behavior.error-behavior:
If a database query during range construction fails, the error is returned to the caller immediately. If a query during message loading fails, the error is returned. No partial results are returned on error.
If the initial clear-event lookup fails, the error is returned and range construction is abandoned. If the agent record lookup (needed to find a parent) fails, the error is returned and the walk terminates with that error.
